userGroupHero.description: Holding our first meetup on International Women's Day 03/08/21 & Started at Arrowhead Research Park to further the community learning, business awareness, student engagement, career opportunities, and general entrepreneurial-garage-hobbyist spirit. We aim to learn from, serve, educate, and entertain the diverse people of the Juarez MX/El Paso + Las Cruces, all of New Mexico, and surrounding rural areas.

helpPanel.communityDescription: AWS UG Cloud Del Norte is a quest to network, experiment, and upskill together. We hold in-person, virtual, and hybrid meetups focusing on regional topics to rural New Mexico, West Texas, Northern Chihuaha, the Borderplex and beyond.
helpPanel.findLocalGroup: Find your local AWS User Group on Meetup.
helpPanel.andresRole: Principal Software Architect, Caylent — AWS Community Builder
helpPanel.communityFounded: In-person, virtual, and hybrid meetups across the Juárez MX / El Paso and Las Cruces corridor, all of New Mexico, and surrounding rural areas. Founded on International Women's Day — March 8, 2021 — at
helpPanel.andresMorenoBioPrefix: Principal Software Architect at Caylent. Serverless specialist — Lambda, Step Functions, CI/CD, and developer experience. Writes at
helpPanel.andresMorenoBioSuffix: on real-world serverless patterns from production systems.
helpPanel.bryanChaskoBio: Builder-focused organizer based in El Paso. AWS Hero recognition for community contributions across the US/MX border region. Creates hands-on content around containers, serverless, and AI/ML infrastructure. Runs the group's technical programming and speaker pipeline.
helpPanel.jacobWrightBio: Co-founded the group in Las Cruces. Anchor for the Doña Ana County.
helpPanel.wayneSavageBioPrefix: Established New Mexico's first AWS User Group, hosting us at
helpPanel.wayneSavageBioMiddle: . Wayne is a champion of funding for entrepreneurs throughout our region & recently brought the
helpPanel.organizersWantedIntro: Cloud del Norte is a volunteer-run group. These roles are open:

helpPanel.lsmLeadDesc: Lengua de Señas Mexicana — conectar la comunidad sorda de Juárez con los eventos del grupo
helpPanel.spanishSpeakersDesc: help reach Spanish-speaking builders across the Borderplex
helpPanel.womenWelcomeDesc: we actively recruit women builders for organizing roles

api.howRiotAPIRESTful: 🌐 How Riot Games API is RESTful
api.title: Cloud Del Norte - API Learning
api.restDescription: REST (REpresentational State Transfer) and API (Application Programming Interface - a way for programs to talk to each other) work together. The Riot Games API demonstrates all 6 REST constraints:
api.uniformInterfaceDesc: Standard HTTP GET method with consistent JSON responses across all resources:
api.clientServerDesc: Our React (web framework) app is the client, Riot's computers are the server. We call their API, they respond with data. Each side can update independently.
api.statelessDesc: Every API call (request) to Riot includes the X-Riot-Token header (our authentication key). No login sessions - each request is complete.
api.cacheableDesc: Riot's Data Dragon CDN (fast global servers) uses version numbers like 13.24.1. Our browser can save champion data and reuse it.
api.layeredSystemDesc: Riot hides their internal structure. We call api.riotgames.com but don't know if it goes through load balancers, databases, or game servers.
api.codeOnDemandDesc: Our React hook fetches data dynamically:
api.codeOnDemandImages: Champion images load from Riot's CDN only when needed.
api.uniformInterfaceDesc2: The same HTTP GET method and JSON format work across different resources:
api.responseExpectedDesc: with JSON array of contest objects:
api.contestsResponseDesc: 🟢 Live contest data from Lambda endpoint - Recent tournament results
api.worldsChampionsDescLong: Performance scores based on tournament KDA, objective control, and team impact metrics
api.performanceScoreDesc: Calculated from tournament KDA, objective control, and team fight impact (0-100 scale).
api.signatureChampionDesc: Most impactful champion played during the tournament run.
api.selectYearToView: Select a year to view championship data
api.liveChampionDataDesc: 🟢 Live champion data from Riot Games Data Dragon API - Real stats updated from League of Legends servers
api.demoChampionDataDesc: 🟡 Demo data (Click "Fetch from Riot API" to load live data and trigger AWS Lambda)
api.availableWithBasicKeyList:
• Data Dragon API: Champion stats, abilities
• Challenger League: Top ranked players
• Champion Mastery: Player expertise
• Match History: Recent game results
api.requiresSpecialAccessList:
• Tournament API: Official esports matches
• Featured Games: Live high-level matches
• Esports Data: Professional results
• Production Keys: Higher rate limits
api.dataSourcesFromRiotList:
• Champion names & lore titles
• Attack damage, health, speed stats
• Official game balance data
api.ourProcessingList:
• Tier rankings (S/A-Tier algorithm)
• Display scaling (÷10, ÷100, ÷20)
• Performance calculations
